metadata.description: Curated ancestry-stratified variant-disease associations. Each entry has both an ancestry-specific OR and a EUR reference OR for AES computation.
removed_v1.1: Lactose Intolerance — rs4988235 entries removed: EUR or=0.45 (protective framing) vs non-EUR ORs 3.2–6.8x (risk framing) produce a direction artifact in the AES formula. PMID 14507249 (cited as Enattah 2002 LCT) resolves to Hashibe 2003 (bladder cancer survival). Entries removed rather than repaired; rs4988235 is near-fixed in EAS (C/C universal) making any OR for persistence meaningless in that population.
removed_v1.3.0: rs8042680 T2D AFR — removed: cited PMID 27005778 (Kettunen 2016 metabolomics GWAS) is wrong paper (wrong phenotype, wrong ancestry); no AFR-specific T2D paper found for this locus
rs1333049 CAD AFR — removed: no African-ancestry CAD replication paper found for 9p21/rs1333049
rs4343 HTN AFR — removed: candidate-gene literature without a clean AFR GWAS; PMID 27005778 is wrong paper
rs699 HTN AFR — removed: same candidate-gene fragmentation issue; no clean AFR paper
rs2981582 BC AFR — removed: AFR-specific FGFR2 breast cancer paper not confirmed; PMID 27005778 wrong
rs10033464 AF AFR — removed: AFR-specific PITX2 AF paper not confirmed; PMID 27005778 wrong
rs1800497 PD AFR — removed: PMID 27005778 wrong paper AND direction conflict (McGuire 2011 PEGASUS shows protective in African-Americans, opposite to stated OR 1.32)
rs10455872 CAD SAS — removed: no SAS-specific LPA/rs10455872 replication paper confirmed; was wrongly cited under Cho 2011 EAS T2D (PMID 22158537)
rs4343 HTN SAS — removed: no SAS hypertension paper found; was wrongly cited under Cho 2011 EAS T2D (PMID 22158537)
rs699 HTN SAS — removed: no SAS hypertension paper found; was wrongly cited under Cho 2011 EAS T2D (PMID 22158537)
rs429358 AD SAS — removed: no SAS-specific APOE Alzheimer's paper confirmed; was wrongly cited under Cho 2011 EAS T2D (PMID 22158537)

associations.note: TCF7L2 strongest T2D signal worldwide; effect slightly larger in AFR (OR 1.46 vs EUR 1.37)
Miyake K et al. 2008 J Hum Genet: rs7903146 in 2,214 Japanese T2D cases/1,873 controls, OR=1.48
TCF7L2 rs7903146 — original EUR discovery study
Kooner et al. 2011 Nat Genet 43:984 SAS T2D GWAS; replication of rs7903146/TCF7L2 in South Asian cohort pending PMC3773920 full-text verification
Omori S et al. 2008 Diabetes 57:791: rs13266634 in 1,630 Japanese cases/1,064 controls, P=0.0073
SLC30A8 original EUR discovery
Kooner et al. 2011 Nat Genet 43:984 SAS T2D GWAS; rs13266634/SLC30A8 SAS replication pending PMC3773920 full-text verification
Yasuda K et al. 2008 Nat Genet 40:1092: original EAS KCNQ1 GWAS discovery, OR=1.49 discovery / 1.40 meta-analysis
Kooner et al. 2011 Nat Genet 43:984 SAS T2D GWAS; rs2237892/KCNQ1 SAS replication pending PMC3773920 full-text verification
Omori S et al. 2008 Diabetes 57:791: rs7756992 CDKAL1 in Japanese cohort, P=0.0363
CDKAL1 EUR discovery
Kooner et al. 2011 Nat Genet 43:984 SAS T2D GWAS; rs7756992/CDKAL1 SAS replication pending PMC3773920 full-text verification
Kooner et al. 2011 Nat Genet 43:984 SAS T2D GWAS; rs1552224/CENTD2 SAS replication pending PMC3773920 full-text verification
CENTD2 EUR discovery
Kooner et al. 2011 Nat Genet 43:984 SAS T2D GWAS; rs8042680/PRC1 SAS replication pending PMC3773920 full-text verification
Kooner et al. 2011 Nat Genet 43:984 SAS T2D GWAS; rs5219/KCNJ11 SAS replication pending PMC3773920 full-text verification
Omori S et al. 2008 Diabetes 57:791: rs5219 KCNJ11 in Japanese cohort, P=0.0034
KCNJ11 EUR discovery study
PROXY — Bhanushali et al. 2013 Genet Res 95:138 direct rs1333049 genotyping in Indian CAD cases/controls (OR 1.564); best available SAS replication, OR does not match exactly
9p21.3 CAD locus: Samani et al. 2007 NEJM WTCCC/German MI study explicitly reports rs1333049 as strongest EUR CAD signal
LPA locus — strong CAD effect; G allele rare in EAS/AFR, most relevant to EUR/SAS
PCSK9 Y142X/C679X LOF in Black participants (9 cohorts): pooled OR=0.51 (0.28-0.92) — matches stated OR 0.50; Circ Cardiovasc Genet 2017 meta-analysis
PCSK9 R46L loss-of-function in ARIC white subjects, HR=0.50 (0.32-0.79) for incident CHD; Cohen et al. 2006 NEJM
PROXY — Niu et al. 2011 J Renin Angiotensin Aldosterone Syst: ACE G2350A meta-analysis. No single landmark GWAS; candidate-gene era association
Jeunemaitre et al. 1992 Cell 71:169 — seminal linkage+association study establishing M235T (rs699) in European hypertension families; pre-GWAS era

APOL1 G1 allele. RISK SCORING: recessive compound model (see compound_recessive_groups). Per-allele log-additive OR is biologically wrong for APOL1 — nephropathy risk is recessive and requires two high-risk alleles (G1+G2, G1/G1, or G2/G2). Genovese et al. (2010) Science 329:841. PMID 20566908.

APOL1 G2 allele. RISK SCORING: recessive compound model (see compound_recessive_groups). Two high-risk alleles (G1+G2, G1/G1, or G2/G2) confer ~7x FSGS/CKD risk. Genovese et al. (2010) Science 329:841. PMID 20566908.
FGFR2 strongest EUR breast cancer GWAS signal; effect weaker in non-EUR populations
Barzan D et al. 2013 Eur J Hum Genet 21:1286: rs2981582 in 984 Chinese cases/2,206 controls, OR=1.20 (1.07-1.34)
TOX3 breast cancer locus — EUR-enriched G allele; attenuated in EAS
Barzan D et al. 2013 Eur J Hum Genet 21:1286: TOX3 locus confirmed significant in Chinese population (same cohort as rs2981582)
PITX2 locus — dominant AF signal in EUR; T allele common in EUR, lower in AFR/EAS
ZFHX3 AF locus — EUR-primary signal

associations.pmid_note: Dubois et al. (2010) Nat Genet 42:295-302 — celiac disease GWAS (confirmed PTPN22 R620W). Previous entry used PMID 17571276 (Cdk5 neurodegeneration paper) which was incorrect. Re-verify against GWAS Catalog.
associations.note: PTPN22 R620W: strong EUR celiac/autoimmune signal; A allele very rare in EAS and AFR

HFE C282Y: recessive model — homozygotes (AA) carry ~9.8x haemochromatosis risk; heterozygous carriers (GA) have clinically benign iron accumulation. Per-allele additive model is biologically wrong for this locus.

associations.pmid_note: Wu et al. (2012) Nat Genet 44:1090-1093 — ESCC GWAS in Chinese populations; ALDH2 locus. Two prior PMIDs were wrong: 20686008 (anaesthesiology case report) and 22561518 (Jin 2012 vitiligo GWAS). PMID requires re-verification against GWAS Catalog accession GCST001563 before a verified PMID can be reinstated.
associations.note: Wu C et al. 2012 Nat Genet Chinese Han ESCC GWAS confirming ALDH2 locus on 12q24; GWAS Catalog GCST001563 (accession-to-PMID mapping unverified directly, flag for confirmation)
APOE e4 (rs429358 C): slightly attenuated Alzheimer's risk in AFR (OR 2.20) vs EUR (OR 3.20) — still major risk factor
APOE e4 landmark EUR Alzheimer's OR per allele
McGuire et al. 2011 J Neurol Sci 307:22 (PEGASUS multiethnic consortium); non-Hispanic white: OR=1.5 for TaqIA/rs1800497 homozygotes and PD

APOL1_CKD.one_allele_note: Single APOL1 high-risk allele (carrier): modest risk elevation. The recessive high-risk state requires two APOL1 risk alleles (G1+G2, G1/G1, or G2/G2).
APOL1_CKD.note: APOL1 compound heterozygote or homozygote (G1+G2, G1/G1, or G2/G2): ~7x risk for focal segmental glomerulosclerosis / CKD. Genovese et al. (2010) Science 329:841. PMID 20647424.

HFE_HH.one_allele_note: Single HFE C282Y allele (carrier): mild biochemical iron accumulation; full clinical haemochromatosis is rare in heterozygotes.
HFE_HH.note: HFE C282Y homozygote: ~9.8x risk for hereditary haemochromatosis (iron overload). Feder et al. (1996) Nat Genet. PMID 9068472. EUR-restricted locus; risk allele effectively absent in EAS/AFR.
